Tuchel doesn't have long left to make a decision on Dowman, with the upcoming March internationals his last chance to take a look at England's options before he begins naming his World Cup squad.

And despite Dowman only being capped up to Under-19 level, talkSPORT's Gabby Agbonlahor claims the poor form of others in his position - namely Phil Foden - should see him considered for selection.

5 Phil Foden looked to have rediscovered his best form at the back end of 2025, but has gone off the boil in recent months Credit: Getty

Responding to Beya's calls, he said: “It’s not the craziest thing I've heard because we talk about number 10s, but are number 10s performing?

“Phil Foden has zero goals in 15 games in 2026. Don't rule it out, you can't rule it out.”

Long hailed as the country's biggest natural talent, Foden struggled with Manchester City in recent months.

After a difficult 2024/25 campaign, which saw Tuchel leave the Manchester City star out of successive England squads, the playmaker enjoyed a resurgence towards the end of 2025 with six goals in four games.

But he has endured another dip in form of late, with only one assist to show for in goal contributions in 2026.

And with Dowman capable of operating in both the No.10 role and out wide, Beya claimed his versatility makes him an even more attractive option to Tuchel.

5 Thomas Tuchel faces a selection headache in the attacking midfield position Credit: Getty

Stating Dowman's case, he continued: "On top of that he can play on the flanks if you really want as well.

"He comes on and he makes a difference. You saw that with the cross from deep for the first goal.

“People are going on like it's wild. Who can come on and cause that much problem for a defence right now in the England team right now? Who, besides Noni Madueke?”

Tuchel will name his squad for the upcoming friendlies with Uruguay and Japan on Friday, March 20.

FIFA recently announced that each participating country must name their final 26-man squad for the World Cup on May 30.

Football.title: Martin Keown defends himself amid accusations of Arsenal bias in fiery Simon Jordan clash
Football.content:
Martin Keown defends himself amid accusations of Arsenal bias in fiery Simon Jordan clash

Martin Keown has hit back at claims he has shown bias towards Arsenal as a pundit this season.

The former defender was accused of a 'lack of objectivity' by Simon Jordan on Monday.

4 Keown has defended himself following accusations of showing bias towards Arsenal Credit: talkSPORT

It followed Jordan's comments following Arsenal's Champions League last 16 first leg against Bayer Leverkusen.



The talkSPORT co-host argued Keown was 'the grand fromage of bias' for his stance on the Gunners' late penalty in Germany.

Mikel Arteta's side equalised in the 89th minute after Noni Madueke had won a spot-kick.

Kai Havertz subsequently stepped up to score from 12 yards.

Keown, who was part of TNT Sports' coverage of the match, agreed with the referee's decision to award a penalty.

Speaking after the match, he revealed that he felt Madueke received enough contact to warrant going down.



Keown responds to bias claims

The former England international joined White & Jordan on Monday and had the accusations put to him.

When given the chance to respond, he began by asking: "I shouldn’t even be asked that sort of question. Does anyone else have that question?"



Keown continued: "They were very respected commentators. Lucy Ward - Pundit of the Year, Adam Summerton, who said they thought it was a penalty.

"I’m not the only one in the room. I’m not the co-commentator.

4 Keown agreed with the decision to award Arsenal a penalty in their draw with Leverkusen Credit: Getty



"I’ve taken all the information on board and I felt that, ok yes, it looks as if his foot gets trapped. So for me, I don’t think that’s being biased. I’ve just seen what I’ve seen.

"There was a penalty at the weekend that wasn’t given against Havertz."

Jordan hit back by replying: "Unfortunately for those guys, they haven’t shared the studio with you as much as we have. So we have seen it, in all its glory."

However, Keown added: "The fact was, though, you were like all those fans, the detractors of Arsenal, who tuned in the 89th minute to say, ‘Arsenal are going to lose, it’s going to blow up.’

"When that didn’t happen, you were like the angry man shouting at a man on the television who was saying, ‘no, no, no, it was a penalty’, because it didn’t go with your narrative."



Arsenal scored late on once again as they beat Everton 2-0 in the Premier League on Saturday.

4 Jordan had claimed Keown showed a 'lack of objectivity' with his remarks Credit: talkSPORT

The Gunners had appeared destined to be held to a goalless draw before Viktor Gyokeres' 89th minute goal.

Max Dowman then added a second in stoppage time to seal all three points.

It is not the first time Keown has been forced to defend himself over his former club this season.



He previously called out talkSPORT's Andy Goldstein for his criticism of the north London club.

However, Keown himself has also been accused of 'killing' the team with his own comments.

Wayne Rooney told the 59-year-old to 'stay silent' for the good of his old side when it came to the Premier League title race.

4 Arsenal's late win over Everton angered their detractors, according to Keown Credit: Getty

As a player, Keown had two spells at Arsenal after initially coming through the club's academy.



Having left the club for Aston Villa in 1986, he returned to Highbury seven years later.

His second spell would last nine years, with Keown making a total of 449 appearances for the Gunners, scoring eight goals from centre-back.

He also won 10 trophies including three Premier League titles and the Cup Winners' Cup.

Football.title: Filipe Luis set for instant return and plunged into immediate relegation battle
Football.content:
Filipe Luis set for instant return and plunged into immediate relegation battle

Filipe Luis is reportedly the leading candidate to become the next Cruzeiro manager, weeks after being sacked by Flamengo.

The ex-Chelsea and Atletico Madrid defender was dismissed by his former club in early March, despite enjoying a hugely successful spell in the Brazilian Serie A.

3 Filipe Luis is a hot property on the managerial market Credit: AFP

This came as a major shock, with Luis collecting a host of major trophies in his 15-month tenure.



Appointed in September 2024, he won the Brazilian Cup two months later before clinching the league title in his first season in charge.

Copa Libertadores glory followed, as well as numerous other Super Cups and silverware, before things came to an abrupt end.

But Luis' trophy-laden stint was cut short just hours after an 8-0 win over Mudureira (11-0 on aggregate), with reports later emerging that he had held secret talks with senior figures at Chelsea over joining sister club Strasbourg.

Time's up for Tite

According to Brazilian outlet Globo, Luis is now the favourite to take the reins at Cruzeiro, after the sacking of Tite.



The outlet claim that Luis is an attractive option, who has previously worked with some players on the team, while he also knows current director of football Bruno Spindel from his time at Flamengo.

Cruzeiro confirmed Tite's dismissal on Monday morning, following a 3-3 draw with Vasco da Gama a day earlier.



This was the top-flight club's third draw in six league games this season, with the other three ending in losses.

Having failed to register a win, the Brazilian Serie A side find themselves in the relegation zone, sitting second from bottom on three points.



And the poor run of form ultimately cost former Brazil boss Tite his job, with the club confirming his dismissal in a statement.

3 Tite has been dismissed by Cruzeiro, despite winning eight of his 17 matches in charge Credit: Getty

It read: "Cruzeiro announces that it has decided to terminate Tite’s contract as the team’s head coach.

"Along with the head coach, assistant coaches Matheus Bachi and Vinicius Bergantin, as well as strength and conditioning coach Fabio Mahseredjian, are also leaving the club.

"We thank them for their service and wish them success in their future endeavours.



"For the time being, Cruzeiro’s first team will be led on an interim basis by Wesley Carvalho, the club’s permanent assistant coach."

Tite took charge at Cruzeiro in December 2025, and managed a total of 17 games, winning eight of them, drawing thrice and losing the remaining six.

His departure comes just a week after he ended Cruzeiro's six-year wait for Campeonato Mineiro glory.

3 Filipe Luis lifted a host of trophies in less than 18 months in charge of Flamengo Credit: Getty

The Belo Horizonte outfit beat Atletico Mineiro to win the State Championship, but the final was marred by on-pitch violence, with a wild brawl seeing an astonishing 23 red cards given out.



Filipe Luis' managerial career so far

In the wake of Tite's sacking, Luis has been tipped for an immediate return to the dugout, and it isn't hard to see why.

After being promoted from his role in charge of Flamengo's Under-20s, the 40-year-old made an instant impact with the senior team.

Luis won the Copa Libertadores in 2025 as he scooped seven trophies in just 101 games for the club - this equates to a piece of silverware roughly every 14 matches.

His exploits also saw him named South American coach of the year in 2025.

Regarded as one of the hottest young coaches on the market, Luis has already been tipped as a future Chelsea and Atletico Madrid manager, with South American football expert Tim Vickery singing his praises to talkSPORT in December.

Football.title: Brentford vs Wolves betting tips and free bets: Three selections for Monday night clash
Football.content:
Brentford vs Wolves betting tips and free bets: Three selections for Monday night clash

It might well be a case of too little too late as a resurgent Wolverhampton Wanderers side head to Brentford in the Monday night Premier League game.

Winning recent games against Aston Villa and Liverpool, Wolves have made a late push for survival but it’s unlikely to be enough to stop the West Midlands outfit from being a Championship side next season.

Those wins over the Villans and the Reds came at Molineux and Wolves have been a different beast on the road, and not in a positive way.

Winless away from home in the Premier League this season, it’s hard to see Wolves getting anything from their trip to the Gtech Community Stadium on Monday night.

GET £40 IN FREE BETS WHEN YOU BET ON THE FOOTBALL WITH talkSPORT BET

Brentford vs Wolves

Brentford to win and both teams to score @ 11/5

Igor Thiago first goalscorer @ 5/2

Keane Lewis-Potter anytime goalscorer @ 9/2

Brentford have tailed off at home in recent weeks but by and large, the Gtech Community Stadium has been a tough place for visiting teams to go this season.

The likes of Manchester United, Liverpool and Newcastle United have all left the home of the Bees empty-handed and it’s unlikely Wolves will have anything to show for their efforts on Monday night either.

Brentford earned a comfortable 2-0 win at Molineux when these sides met in the Premier League earlier this season.

Wolves’ recent resurgence and Brentford’s slight dip in home form might make this a slightly tighter contest, with both sides capable of getting on the scoresheet.

However, Brentford’s extra quality, especially in the final third, should show through over the course of the game and Keith Andrews’ men are tipped for the win.

It has been a tremendous Premier League campaign for Brentford forward Igor Thiago, with the Brazilian having already scored 18 goals this season.

Scoring a brace in the Bees’ FA Cup defeat to West Ham United last time out, Thiago has now bagged three goals in his last three games.

Scoring first-half goals against both the Hammers and Burnley in that time, Thiago has been a threat from the first whistle and the 24-year-old is good value to bag the opening goal of the game against Wolves in this one.

Keane Lewis-Potter has been a key figure for Andrews in the Brentford midfield this season and the 25-year-old has also shown an eye for goal.

Able to pick up pockets of space around the area, Lewis-Potter’s last goal came last month in Brentford’s 1-1 draw against Premier League frontrunners Arsenal.

The former Hull City star will have fond memories of facing Wolves, having scored both goals in the 2-0 victory at Molineux back in December.

That was a day to remember for Lewis-Potter and, knowing how he can get himself in goalscoring positions against this opponent, the Englishman is well priced to bag a fourth Premier League goal of the season on Monday night.

Football.content:
Brentford's European push continues when they host Wolves in West London on Monday night and that is the pick of the action as the new week starts.

However, there is also action in the Championship, La Liga and Serie A to keep an eye on.

We have a trio of selections across Monday’s coupon which make up our best bets of the day.

GET £40 IN FREE BETS WHEN YOU BET ON THE FOOTBALL WITH talkSPORT BET

Monday football tips

Bet of the Day - Over 3.5 goals in Brentford v Wolves @ 7/4

Brentford and Wolves meet in West London on Monday night and the Premier League duo should deliver an entertaining contest.

The Bees continue to push for European qualification and there isn't much possibility of Keith Andrews' men sitting back on home turf.

Wolves have shown plenty of spirit in recent weeks and Rob Edwards' men have scored six goals in their last three top-flight games, against Arsenal, Aston Villa and Liverpool.

Brentford have seen seven of their 14 home Premier League games go over 3.5 goals and the Bees have found the net 24 times on their own patch.

That's an impressive effort from the Premier League's bottom club and backing over 3.5 goals rates a cracking play in west London.

Special of the Day - Igor Thiago 2+ shots on target & Kevin Schade 1+ shots on target in Brentford v Wolves bet builder @ 7/4

Brentford’s free-flowing attacking play has been a feature of the Premier League season and forwards Igor Thiago and Kevin Schade have been leading the charge for the Bees.

Thiago is averaging 1.31 shots on target per 90 minutes in the Premier League this season, while Schade is operating at 0.91.

Clearly, there can be an expectation of improvement on those numbers against the Premier League’s bottom club and Wolves have conceded 21 shots on target in their last three matches.

Take Thiago to have 2+ shots on target and Schade to hit at least one in a 7/4 bet builder.

Longshot of the Day - Cremonese to beat Fiorentina, Rayo Vallecano to beat Levante & Portsmouth to beat Derby treble @ 12/1

There is a key relegation battle in Serie A this evening when Cremonese host Fiorentina and the home side could leave La Viola blue.

It's been a remarkable fall for Fiorentina, who finished sixth in the Italian top-flight last term, but who have won only two of 14 away league matches this season and are deep in trouble.

Cremonese have lost only five of their 13 home Serie A matches and they may resist the visitors, who had midweek European commitments.

Levante have taken only four points from their last six La Liga matches and that's a run which has dragged the Frogs into a relegation battle.

Rayo Vallecano have lost only two of their 17 matches at fortress Campo de Futbol de Vallecas and therefore rate as home bankers here.

Portsmouth can boost their Championship survival hopes by sailing to victory against Derby. The Rams have lost three second-tier away games on the bounce and John Eustace's men had only one shot on target at Millwall last time out.

Football.title: Roy Keane accused of having agenda with scathing verdict on Manchester United interim Michael Carrick
Football.content:
Roy Keane accused of having agenda with scathing verdict on Manchester United interim Michael Carrick

Michael Carrick can guide Manchester United to 10-0 wins for the remainder of the season and still Roy Keane wouldn't want him in charge.

That's the view of ex-Aston Villa striker Gabby Agbonlahor, who believes the Irishman has an agenda against the interim Red Devils boss.

4 Carrick has guided United into the Champions League places having impressed as interim boss Credit: Getty

Carrick was placed in charge at Old Trafford for the rest of the campaign in January following the sacking of Ruben Amorim.



It was the second time that United have turned to their former midfielder after the 44-year-old enjoyed a three-game unbeaten spell as caretaker in late 2021.

And he has once again impressed this time around - guiding the club up into third spot having won seven of his nine matches in the dugout.

While United co-owner Jim Ratcliffe is remaining coy on Carrick landing the job on a full-time basis, a large majority of fans are backing the board to keep him on.

One of his biggest supporters is ex-teammate Wayne Rooney, who has insisted Carrick must have his temporary stay turned into a permanent deal following Sunday's 2-1 win over Villa.



Don't take the Mich

Though fellow United icon Keane is against that idea, as the Sky Sports pundit insisted there are better options out there than Carrick.

Keane declared that Carrick is still inexperienced at the top level of management, as he stated Paris Saint-Germain's Luis Enrique and Atletico Madrid's Diego Simeone should be potential candidates.



His opinion was a subject of discussion on the Sports Breakfast show on Monday morning.

Commenting on Keane's view, Agbonlahor mentioned that the midfielder may be 'bitter' that Jonny Evans and Jonathan Woodgate are part of Carrick's backroom team.



While he also highlighted Keane's public spat with Carrick's wife, which stems back to 2014, as another reason why he may not want him in charge.

4 Keane believes there are better options out there for the Red Devils than Carrick Credit: Shutterstock Editorial

4 United beat Villa 3-1 on Sunday to go three points clear of Unai Emery's men Credit: AFP

He said: "You need someone that's neutral to speak about it.

"Wayne Rooney's his good friend, Roy Keane and him have fell out obviously because he brought up his his wife recently.

"And he was so bitter and angry about himself about Johnny Evans getting a job and Woodgate.



"So there's no point listening to what Roy Keane's got to say about that situation.

"Because Michael Carrick could win every game 10-0 and he'd say, 'Don't give it Michael Carrick,' so your pointless asking Keane."

Though Agbonlahor did stress that he would only hand the keys to Carrick full-time once the season is over - and only if he secures Champions League qualification.

4 Carrick's only loss this season came away at Newcastle earlier this month

He remarked: "What if they give it to him now and then their results become poor?



"And then they don't finish top five, everyone's saying, 'Why not wait until the end of the season?'

"If he continues as he is, I would give it to him at the end of the season.

"But I would give him a one or two-year deal, not a three, four or five-year deal, one or two years, he will take that."

It was a view shared by co-host Ally McCoist, who believes no domestic cup action and European football is benefiting Carrick.

He stated: "Yeah I'm with you, I understand the clamour of giving the job right now, but why would you?



"I think things are ticking along nicely, give him the opportunity to continue, I've mentioned the one game a week which helps, there's no doubt about that.

"I think they will finish in the Champions League places and I actually do think he will get the job, and I think he probably should get the job.

"Not probably, I think he should get the job on the level of performance that his team's producing, on the level of improvement."

United now turn their attention to facing Bournemouth at the Vitality Stadium on Friday, where victory would briefly extend their lead over fourth-placed Villa to six points.

News.title: Iran’s maimed & missing Supreme Leader ‘is probably gay & had relationship with his male teacher, stunned Trump is told’
News.content:
IRAN’s maimed and missing Supreme Leader may be gay and had a romantic relationship with his male teacher, reports say.

Donald Trump was reportedly left stunned and laughing out loud after learning that US intelligence suspects Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei is gay.





The intel also indicates that Mojtaba’s slain father Ali Khamenei opposed his son succeeding him – precisely due to fears over his sexuality, the New York Post reported.

Top Washington aides reportedly found the report “hilarious” and laughed alongside the US president.

One senior intelligence official “has not stopped laughing about it for days”, a source told the outlet.

At least three sources told The Post that the sensational allegation is in fact seen as credible by US spy agencies – and not a false piece of information intended to poke fun at Mojtaba.

Two of the sources said that Mojtaba, 56, who is nicknamed “the power behind the robes” had a long-term sexual relationship with a childhood tutor.

The third source alleged that the new Ayatollah had an affair with a person who used to work for the Khamenei family, according to the intel.

It comes after The Sun revealed that MIA Mojtaba is likely in a coma and missing one leg after being critically wounded in a US-Israeli strike.

Secretary of War Pete Hegseth has also said the newly elected Supreme Leader was “likely disfigured” in the February 28 attack which killed his dad and other relatives.



Mojtaba is believed to have made “aggressive” sexual overtures to men caring for him – possibly while affected by heavy medication, one source said.

There is no physical evidence that the new Ayatollah is gay – but all sources highlighted that the tip was rock solid.

One of them said the evidence was “derived from one of the most protected sources that the government has”.

Another said: “The fact that this was elevated to the highest of high levels shows you there’s some confidence in this.”

And the third said: “His father and others suspected he was gay and that was something that people were spreading to try to stop his ascension.”



Rumours around Mojtaba’s sexual orientation have swirled since at least May 2024.

The whispers have been “a pretty closely held piece of information”, one source claimed.

Trump has called Mojtaba a “lightweight” leader, while Israel has vowed to eliminate whoever takes the throne in the wake of Khamenei Snr’s death.



It also comes after reports emerged that Mojtaba had been treated for impotency in London multiple times, according to a classified 2008 US cable.

Despite the intel claiming he visited a clinic while facing pressure to have an heir, it did not speculate on what may have caused the condition.

Mojtaba’s alleged homosexuality was hinted at in a CBS News report on Sunday that said the late Ayatollah Khamenei had reservations about his son taking power.

The slain Supreme Leader wanted a different successor due to unspecified “issues” in Mojtaba’s “personal life”.

Homosexual conduct is illegal in Iran – but the government does allow surgical sex change operations.



Some gay men are reportedly pressured into going under the knife to avoid criminal penalties.

Sodomy, or anal sex, is also a capital offense in Iran.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Several gay Iranians have been publicly hanged to deter others.

It is currently unclear where Mojtaba is despite the regime releasing his first statement as Supreme Leader last week.

News.title: Chilling moment cleaner pours toxic chemicals into her new colleague’s TEA in sinister workplace poison plot
News.content:
THIS is the harrowing moment a cleaner pours toxic chemicals into her new colleague’s tea as she is caught on camera in a workplace poison plot.

Chilling footage shows the woman browsing a cleaning shelf and picking out a bright green bottle of chemicals.





She then sprays the toxic substance into a tea cup, mixing it into the tea.

Footage shows the woman appearing to wipe her fingerprints off a teaspoon, and repeats the poisoning over and over again.

The woman, Małgorzata W., 56, was given a six year prison sentence today in Poland and slapped with a huge fine.



Her victim, a 51-year-old co-worker, suspected something was wrong when her unattended drinks starting changing colour and tasting strange.

Repeated poisoning left Małgorzata’s colleague with sever internal burns.

Realising something was wrong, she set up a hidden camera to catch Małgorzata in the act.

Chilling CCTV footage shows how Małgorzata was caught red-handed.



The poison plotter was found guilty last week of attempting to cause serious bodily harm.

Judges at Warsaw’s district court found that she had secretly laced her collegues drinks with chemicals over a six-week period.

The twisted poison plot unfolded between November 15 and December, 27, 2024.

After the co-worker drank the tainted tea, she suffered a series of disturbing symptoms.

They included stomach pain, breathing difficulties, a burning sensation in her throat and fainting.



Doctors later said she had suffered serious internal chemical burns, affecting the mucous membranes of her mouth, oesophagus and stomach.

Tests confirmed that cleaning products and other detergents had been added to the teas.

Experts warned that the consequences of drinking such dangerous chemicals could have been fatal.

In one recording, the poisoner can be heard chatting on the phone to a friend, telling her ominously: “The tea is standing there, so you know what that means.”

Moments later, Małgorzata adds a toxic substance to her colleague’s drink.



Another woman, Elzbieta D, was aquitted after prosecutors claimed she knew about the poison plot but did not raise the alarm.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Investigators said the women worked for a cleaning company servicing a Polish Radio building on Mysliwiecka Street in Warsaw.

They said tensions grew after the victim began pointing out the poisoner’s mistakes at work.

News.title: Woman and two men arrested over death of man found in council wheelie bin after ‘hit-and-run’
News.content:
BIN 'MURDER' Woman and two men arrested over death of man found in council wheelie bin after ‘hit-and-run’

A WOMAN and two men have been arrested after a man’s body was discovered in a council wheelie bin after an alleged hit-and-run.

Cops confirmed a man in his 20s and a man and a woman in their 40s had been taken into custody in connection with the grim discovery.





All three were arrested overnight in Blackpool on suspicion of murder and assisting an offender.

They remain in police custody at this time as a probe into the incident continues.

The investigation was launched after a member of the public called police to Daimler Road in Coventry.

Responding officers discovered the body of a man aged between 40 and 50 in a wheelie bin which had been dragged into nearby Cash’s Park.



It’s believed the man may have been hit by a vehicle before being moved in the bin to a muddy path in the park, police said.

West Midlands Police are working to formally identify the man.

The force is also working alongside the local authority to try and establish where the bin had been taken from.

Alongside images of the wheelie bin, police have also released further information about the man to help identify him, and hopefully determine the cause of death.



The man has a tattoo of a cross with a snake wrapped around it on his back and the words “Little Stardust.”

He also has a tattoo on his right arm saying “nan” with a clover and the colours of the Irish flag, cops said.

Police are urging anyone with information, or dashcam or CCTV footage from the area, to reach out.

Detective Chief Inspector Phil Poole, from West Midlands Police’s Major Crime Unit, said: “After beginning our investigation this weekend, we have made a number of arrests overnight.

“All of these people remain in custody at this time while we continue to build a picture of what might have happened.



“Thank you to everyone who has been in touch providing information so far.

“We are continuing to appeal to anyone who might know more about what happened and whether they can help our investigation.”

News.title: Brit murder suspect who ‘severely burnt kidnap victim before dumping him 100 miles away’ caught after six years on run
News.content:
SUSPECT SNARED Brit murder suspect who ‘severely burnt kidnap victim before dumping him 100 miles away’ caught after six years on run

A BRITISH murder suspect who allegedly left a kidnap victim with severe burns before dumping the body 100 miles away has been caught after a six-year manhunt.

Kevin Leon Wooden, 46, is accused of abducting Tomasz Samel, 45, in 2019 – before fleeing the country after a £10,000 bounty was put on his head.





Dominican Republic cops have now extradited Wooden to the UK six years after West Midlands Police released a photo of the suspect while appealing for information leading to his arrest.

Polish national Samel suffered 75 per cent burns after being allegedly kidnapped by two men posing as Interpol officers outside his home in Handsworth, Birmingham.

He was taken and dumped alive more than 100 miles away from the pick-up point, before stumbling to a house in Bickerstaffe, Lancashire, calling for help.

After being rushed to hospital with severe injuries, he died three months later on June 21, 2019.

Cops called the attack “one of the most horrific” they had ever encountered.

Dominican Republic police revealed they had arrested Wooden in July 2025.

He was reportedly held on an international arrest warrant issued by Mexican authorities – who were hunting him over a separate alleged child kidnapping.

This warrant related to Wooden disappearing with his Colombian-born daughter Khadija Alexandria Alvarez Bonilla, now four, when she was just seven months old.



At the time of his arrest, local authorities said a Mexican woman had also been held and that three children were “rescued” – but they did not confirm if one of them was Khadija.

Dominican Republic cops have now said: “British citizen Kevin Leon Wooden has been extradited to the United Kingdom.

“He was sought by the judicial authorities of his country for his alleged involvement in several serious offences, including conspiracy to cause grievous bodily harm, unlawful deprivation of liberty, concealment, kidnapping, homicide and murder.

They added: “The 46-year-old British national was transported on British Airways flight No. BA2204, bound for London, where he will face the judicial authorities on the charges against him.”

Shortly after Samel’s tragic death, Detective Chief Inspector Anastasia Miller of West Midlands Police said: “This was a shocking attack and certainly one of the most horrific in my professional experience.



She added: “Tomasz had been tortured and had received 75 per cent burns to his body.

“Despite being very ill he was able to knock on a door and seek help.

“We had spoken to Tomasz while he was in hospital but he was very unwell.



“We can’t disclose what was said before he died. We’ve been working hard to understand exactly what happened on that day and why.”

West Midlands Police previously said in June 2022 that a 44-year-old suspect had been arrested in Thessaloniki, Greece.

Cops said they would be liaising with Greek authorities and other agencies.

Reports at the time said a number of people had been held as part of the investigation but that no one was charged.



When Wooden was cuffed last year, local police said: “The National Police, through its INTERPOL National Central Bureau (NCB) in Santo Domingo, in coordination with the Public Prosecutor’s Office and with the support of international agencies, captured a British citizen in the National District who was wanted by the Mexican judicial authorities for the crime of child abduction.”

They added: “The detainee is Kevin Leon Wooden and/or Shaban Kuka, 45, who used false Mexican identity documents under the name Miguel Antonio Torres Hernández.

“The arrest took place on Roberto Pastoriza Avenue, at the corner of Abraham Lincoln, in the National District.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

“Wooden is wanted on an INTERPOL Red Notice for international search and arrest, accused of ‘child abduction,’ in addition to having a criminal record for various offences in his country of origin.



“During the raid, carried out at a house in the Bella Vista neighbourhood, three children were rescued, and 41-year-old Mexican citizen Viviana Villarreal Cambero was also arrested.”

News.title: Mum accused of murdering newborn baby girl who plunged to her death from window appears in court
News.content:
TOT TRAGEDY Mum accused of murdering newborn baby girl who plunged to her death from window appears in court

A MUM has appeared in court accused of murdering her newborn baby daughter after she plunged to her death from a window.

The 18-day-old was rushed to hospital following the horror at a residential home in Westminster, central London, on Saturday morning.





Tragically, she could not be saved and was declared dead shortly after.

Zahira Byjaouane has now appeared at Westminster Magistrates’ Court charged with murder.

The 43-year-old spoke only to confirm her name, date of birth and address during the brief hearing.



She was remanded into custody to next appear at the Old Bailey on March 18.

Byjaouane waved to loved ones in the public gallery as she entered and left the dock of the court.

Police were scrambled to the Peabody Estate at roughly 7.30am on Saturday.

Forensic officers were later seen combing for clues at a window in a flat on the building’s third floor.



Byjaouane was arrested at the scene and taken into police custody.

On her LinkedIn profile, Byjaouane says she worked as a clinical co-ordinator in Harley Street between July 2024 and April 2025.

She also claims to have worked as a medical lab assistant in various laboratories, including at St Mary’s Hospital, Paddington.

The London Ambulance Service said the baby was treated at the scene before being taken to a major trauma centre.

Floral tributes and toys have been left on the street, which is home to government buildings, including the Department for Transport‘s offices, as well as Channel 4‘s headquarters.



Detectives from the Met’s Specialist Crime Command are leading the investigation.

Detective Chief Inspector Alison Foxwell, who is leading the Met’s investigation, said: “The death of an infant is always an extremely tragic occurrence, and officers are making urgent inquiries around the circumstances.

“At this stage, we believe the incident occurred within a domestic context, but we are appealing for anybody with information to come forward.”

Anybody with information can call 101, quoting reference 1596/14MAR.

News.title: Should PM do more to help Brits facing soaring bills due to Iran war? VOTE NOW
News.content:
POLL OF THE DAY Should PM do more to help Brits facing soaring bills due to Iran war? VOTE NOW

SIR Keir Starmer today announced an emergency £53 million support package for heating oil customers.

The PM said the funds will go to “those households that are most exposed” to eye-watering price hikes as turmoil in the Middle East drives up global oil prices.



The targeted financial support will be directed at low-income households in rural communities.



Around 1.7 million households in England and Wales depend on kerosene for heating and hot water.

Some households have seen prices double, with reports of cancelled deliveries and shortages.

But, do you think the PM is doing enough to help Brits with soaring heating oil prices? Have your say in our exclusive poll.

Love to debate? Join Sun Club for just £1.99 a month for the boldest opinion on the issues shaping Britain.

News.title: Tourist, 28, dies after plunging down cliff & into sea while trying to grab his glasses that fell off in Spanish resort
News.content:
DEADLY FALL Tourist, 28, dies after plunging down cliff & into sea while trying to grab his glasses that fell off in Spanish resort

A TOURIST has fallen to his death from a cliff in a Brit holiday hotspot.

The 28-year-old plunged 30ft to his death in Spain while trying to grab his glasses that had fallen off.





The Colombian man was walking with his partner in Pilar de la Horadada, Alicante when tragedy struck.

It is thought he lost his footing as he bent down to retrieve his glasses.

The tourist then tumbled 30ft off the cliff.



He landed in the sea, and his lifeless body was later recovered by police divers.

The path is popular with walkers, but is known for being very steep, with no guard rails between the trail and a sheer drop of the Rubio ravine.

Sources said the accident took place around 7pm on Sunday, when the man was walking the clifftop path with his partner.

He had been strolling path near the Manoli Hotel when he plummeted to his death.



A group as passing cyclists tried to help after his desperate partner raised the alarm.

Police divers recovered the body and an investigation was opened into the tourist’s death.

It comes after a terrifying video emerged showing a glam cycling influencer falling down a sheer cliff face in Cartagena, Spain.

Cecilia Sopeña can be seen clinging onto the rocks for dear life in the horrifying footage.

The ex-adult model was trying to pass her beloved dog Nezha on a mountainous path in Cartagena, Spain, when she skidded off the edge of the slope.



She reportedly left the modelling industry to dedicate herself to her cycling passion, and was riding her bike along the path when her furry friend suddenly doubled back.

Trying to avoid hitting Nezha, Cecilia stumbled and lost her balance.

In the footage taken from her 360-degree helmet camera and posted on Thursday, Cecilia makes a terrifying tumble down the rocky cliff face.

She tries to hold onto the path’s edge but her fingers grab at crumbling rock, with her bike still between her legs.

RECOMMENDED STORIES

Skidding down the sharp rocks on her back, the bike falls away from her.



Eventually, Cecilia is able to stop herself from skidding down further and comes to a painful stop on the cliff face.

News.title: Brit tourist, 39, drowns after being pulled out to sea by deadly rip current on holiday island Bali
News.content:
SEA TRAGEDY Brit tourist, 39, drowns after being pulled out to sea by deadly rip current on holiday island Bali

A BRIT tourist has drowned after being pulled out to sea by a deadly rip current while on holiday in Bali.

The 39-year-old was swimming at Nyang-Nyang Beach when he is believed to have been struck by the swell and pulled away by a rip current.





He was already lifeless when shocked beachgoers discovered his body floating near the shore of the Pecatu village at around 7.45am local time on March 15.

Footage shows two tourists braving the strong waves as they battled to pull the man’s body ashore.

Paramedics arrived and confirmed that he was dead before taking him to the local hospital for a post-mortem examination.

Nyoman Sidakarya, Head of the Denpasar Search and Rescue Office, said: “The search and rescue standby officer on duty received information from the Pecatu community officer requesting evacuation assistance regarding the discovery of a body in the middle of the sea.

“The evacuation involved several agencies, including the Denpasar Search and Rescue Office, the Water Police, South Kuta Police, Pecatu village authorities, Balawista South Kuta lifeguards, and members of the local community.”

Officers carried the Brit to an evacuation point near the beach after he was recovered from the water.

An ambulance then took the man to Prof. Ngoerah General Hospital in Denpasar for verification and examination.

Authorities said they were investigating the cause of his death.



Bali is one of the world’s most popular surfing and swimming destinations.

But rip-tides and unexpectedly powerful waves make it a difficult spot for beginners and inexperienced swimmers.

The Sun has contacted the Foreign Office (FCD0) for comment.

Last week, two Brit holidaymakers died in a tragic drowning at a popular Australian beach as beachgoers desperately tried to save them.

Paramedics were called to Shellharbour Beach in New South Wales on Wednesday following reports of two swimmers struggling in the water.



Beachgoers tried to save a 66-year-old man and 64-year-old woman, but they were sadly pronounced dead at the scene.

News.title: King Charles and Andrew 'will never be seen together again' as true 'resentment' laid bare
News.content:
King Charles and Andrew 'will never be seen together again' as true 'resentment' laid bare

King Charles and Andrew’s relationship has collapsed to an irreparable low, with royal insiders claiming the brothers “will never be seen together again”

View 3 Images HELLO claimed that the estrangement is irreparable (Image: Getty)

The rift between King Charles and his younger brother, Andrew Mountbatten-Windsor, has reached its deepest point, insiders claim, and the brothers are unlikely to ever be seen together in public again. The conflict escalated last month when the King offered his “wholehearted support and co-operation” to the authorities investigating Andrew on suspicion of misconduct in public office.

Andrew was detained following allegations that surfaced after the release of files connected to convicted sex offender Jeffrey Epstein. The claims relate to assertions that Andrew shared sensitive information with Epstein during his tenure as the UK’s trade envoy.

The former prince’s long-standing friendship with the late convicted sex offender had already prompted Charles to strip Andrew of the last of his royal titles in October and relocate him from the Royal Lodge in Windsor to a cottage on the Sandringham estate.

“These censures are deemed necessary, notwithstanding the fact that he continues to deny the allegations against him,” Buckingham Palace said in a statement that did not even refer to Andrew as the King’s brother. “Their Majesties wished to make clear that their thoughts and emotions remain with the victims and survivors of any and all forms of abuse.”

View 3 Images Andrew and King Charles leave following a Catholic funeral service (Image: Adrian Dennis/Getty Images)

He spent approximately 11 hours in custody before being released under investigation. The arrest came despite Andrew having previously denied any wrongdoing in connection with Epstein. While he has strongly refuted past allegations concerning his links to the disgraced financier, he has not directly addressed the most recent claims.

HELLO, which interviewed multiple royal biographers, claimed that the estrangement is irreparable and that Charles and Andrew are unlikely to be seen together in public again, even at family Christmases at Sandringham. Andrew Lownie, author of Entitled: The Rise and Fall of the House of York, told HELLO that the resentment runs so deep that “I don’t think we’ll see Andrew at Charles’s funeral”.

Lownie adds that tensions existed long before Andrew’s association with Epstein. He said: "Andrew resented Charles. He felt that he could do a better job. Charles resented Andrew. He felt that Andrew was allowed to do things he couldn't do." One such source of friction was Andrew being allowed to fly missions in the Falklands War while Charles was kept away from naval service. “Andrew is not a forgiving type. He feels that Charles has thrown his family under a bus to cover the problem," Lownie added.

Robert Jobson, author of The Windsor Legacy: A Royal Dynasty of Secrets, Scandal and Survival, claims the King feels betrayed by revelations from the so-called “Epstein files” released by US authorities.

View 3 Images Andrew has been living in solitude as King Charles has imposed strict rules following arrest. (Image: Daniel Leal/Getty Images)

“In around 2013 or 2014, Charles and Andrew had a massive conversation about Epstein, and Andrew gave him his complete and utter assurance that he had done nothing wrong,” Jobson says, citing a source close to the King. “He gave unequivocal reassurances.”

The estrangement has been visible in public. Senior royals, including the Prince and Princess of Wales, attended Westminster Abbey last week for the Commonwealth Day service, their first joint engagement since Andrew’s arrest. Led by the King, they ignored protesters who held placards referring to the former prince, who has not attended the service since 2019.

Article continues below

Charles and Andrew have not been seen together in public since the funeral of the Duchess of Kent in September, when the King did not acknowledge him, and Andrew’s attempts to engage Prince William were met with stiff nods.

News.title: King of the Decks: King Charles tries his hand at DJing during a visit to Manchester
News.content:
King of the Decks: King Charles tries his hand at DJing during a visit to Manchester

King Charles proved he’s a dab hand on the decks today as he tried his hand at DJing during a visit to Manchester.

The monarch was given a crash course on the DJ decks by Christian St Louis while touring Aviva Studios in the north-west of England.

Watching closely, Charles appeared to enjoy the moment as he was shown how to mix tracks on the equipment.

As he left, Charles unveiled a plaque before greeting members of the community gathered outside, including a group of toddlers he paused to chat with.

The King also visited the Renew Hub, a reuse centre in the Trafford Park, where he also had a go at restoring a chair.

News.title: B&M's 50p vase that looks like £69 John Lewis option 'adds luxury to your home'
News.content:
B&M's 50p vase that looks like £69 John Lewis option 'adds luxury to your home'

Shoppers can achieve a similar look at home for less at B&M

View 3 Images The B&M vase is now down to just 50p online and in stores (Image: Getty)

B&M is selling a low-priced vase that could rival a more expensive version at John Lewis. For shoppers hoping to achieve a 'luxurious' and 'modern' look in their home, B&M's affordable option could provide a similar aesthetic to an item that costs almost 140 times more.

‌



By opting for B&M's budget-friendly Small Ceramic Vase for 50p, shoppers could save £68.50 compared to John Lewis' Neptune Leven Round Stoneware Bud Vase, which costs £69. While there are some differences between the two vases, they still share similarities in shape and design, which could make B&M's option an ideal alternative for interior design fans wanting a 'minimalist' feel at home for as cheaply as possible.

‌



B&M's Small Ceramic Vase was £1, but thanks to a 50% discount, it is now as low as 50p. It is available in stores and online; however, the vase is 'selling fast' as B&M states: "Hurry, low stock".

‌



In the product description, B&M says: "Add a sense of luxury to your home interior with this Small Ceramic Vase. Fill it with your favourite flowers or leave free-standing for that minimalist touch."

B&M points out that the vase is "subject to availability" in stores and advises customers that stock "in your nearest store is not guaranteed and some products are available in selected stores only".

View 3 Images B&M's Small Ceramic Vase (Image: B&M)

‌



On John Lewis' website, the product description for the £69 vase reads: "Neptune's Leven ceramic vase is crafted from hardwearing stoneware in Portugal, where skilled artisans hand-apply a reactive glaze. The glaze combines a glossy and natural finish to showcase the organic shape of each vase. The process creates slight variations from piece to piece, making each vase unique."

It adds: "Combining function and beauty, the vase is perfect for adding a decorative touch to shelves or corners of rooms. It looks just as beautiful on its own as it does holding stems and twigs."

Both vases feature a round neck, and a duo-tone beige colour design. B&M's version is described as 'small' and there aren't any size dimensions listed online. The John Lewis vase is 18cm tall. While John Lewis' option is much more high-end with "skilled artisans" behind its creation, B&M's 50p option is likely mass-produced.

Article continues below

View 3 Images John Lewis' Neptune Leven Round Stoneware Bud Vase (Image: John Lewis)

Elsewhere at B&M, the store is selling a Blown Glass Vase for £5. It is described as a "great way to enhance your decor" at home. B&M says it is "a beautiful glass vase with a stylish flame-effect design". The store continues: "Add your favourite flowers to this vase or use it as a standalone decoration for your home."

A similar-looking pattern appears on a John Lewis vase, selling for £30 online. The Tall Confetti Glass Vase stands at 25cm tall and features a slightly different neck than the B&M version - but the pattern on the outside appears almost identical, with shades of orange, yellow and brown blended together.

News.title: 'Loved' teenager dies after collapsing at K-Pop Demon Hunters rave
News.content:
A "hilarious”, popular university student died after suddenly collapsing at a Halloween rave after she was seen taking the drug MDMA.

Olivia Wojciechowska, 19, was attending a K-Pop Demon Hunters-themed party when she suffered an apparent seizure and was rushed to hospital after taking the party drug, an inquest was told.

Ensure our latest headlines always appear at the top of your Google Search by making us a Preferred Source. Click here to activate or add us as your Preferred Source in your Google search settings.

Her condition deteriorated and she was transferred from the Royal Liverpool Hospital to Queen Elizabeth Hospital in Birmingham, where she died on November 7, 2025, the Liverpool Echo reports.

Her friend Scarlett Tonna said: "She was hilarious, she was always the life of the party. She'd always love going out and being involved in all the socials. She was in the Cheer Society at John Moores and she'd go out with them and dress up."

Image: Liverpool Echo) Liverpool Echo)

Another friend, Niamh Bosher, added: "Her first week she came as a bumble bee. Then she came as a pineapple with these massive feathers on her head. She always loved to dress up and go out."

Scarlet said: "She loved uni, she had so many friends here. She was loved by so many people. (Losing her) was awful for everyone. We had a memorial walk a few days after she passed away and there were hundreds and hundreds of people there who knew her and cared about her.

"We did a fundraiser and thousands of people donated to it, and it racked up quite a lot of money. She was honestly so popular and everyone loved her."

An inquest at Liverpool Coroners' Court today, Monday (March 16), heard how Olivia, a second year business student at Liverpool John Moores University, had attended the Halloween rave at Blackstone Street Warehouse in Vauxhall on November 1 last year. Before the event, she took a taxi to Scarlett's house, where she revealed she had MDMA (ecstasy) in her possession.

The pair arrived at the "The Upside Down: Rise of the Demon Hunters" rave at around 4pm, and Scarlett said she saw Olivia take MDMA around one to two hours later.

Another member of the group said they saw Olivia consuming MDMA before she collapsed at around 10.05pm. She was taken to a welfare tent, where staff used bags of ice to try to bring her temperature down. An ambulance was called and she was taken to Royal Liverpool Hospital, arriving at 11.45pm.

She was taken to the emergency department, where she was intubated and placed on a ventilator. Her liver and kidney functions deteriorated and she was given medication and placed on dialysis.

On November 3 she was approved for a liver transplant, and was transferred to the Queen Elizabeth Hospital in Birmingham on November 5. However, a CT scan that day "confirmed that sadly Olivia was unlikely to survive", and she died two days later before the transplant could be carried out.

A post-mortem examination found her cause of death was multi-organ failure, acute liver failure, and drug intoxication. A registered organ donor, Olivia donated her lungs and her heart, saving the lives of two other people.

Recording a conclusion of a drug-related death, coroner Helen Rimmer said: "Olivia did donate her lungs and her heart prior to her death, and she has given that gift of life to at least two individuals and she has saved two other people.

"I accept the medical cause of death as multi-organ failure, acute liver failure and drug intoxication. She had consumed ecstasy at a rave event in Liverpool on November 1 with fatal consequences."

Following her death, Olivia was repatriated to Northern Ireland, where her funeral took place at St Colmcille's Church, East Belfast, on December 12 last year.

Niamh said: "It seemed like every time you'd go out with Liv, every five minutes you'd stop and talk to someone. She knew everyone and everyone knew her.

"On her birthday we went down the docks and laid flowers down, and a few other people went down too. The Cheer Society's doing a charity event for the Kevin Bell Repatriation Trust that helped her get home. Everybody still thinks about her and they're still planning things."

News.title: 'Grim' UK city transformed from horror deaths hotspot to superstar paradise
News.content:
EXCLUSIVE: 'Grim' UK city transformed from horror deaths hotspot to superstar paradise

New Order, Happy Mondays, Stone Roses... A new BBC series looks back at the groundbreaking 'Madchester' music scene that transformed the city in the 1980s and 1990s

View 5 Images Bez of the Happy Mondays, with wide eyes. Dancing on stage, 1989. (Image: Universal Images Group via Getty Images)

It’s been 50 years since childhood pals Bernard Sumner and Peter Hook were so inspired by a Sex Pistols gig that they formed a band with mate Terry Mason, who had also seen the show. By early 1978 the band’s name had been changed from Warsaw to Joy Division, after the sexual slavery wing of a Nazi concentration camp, with a line-up now consisting of Ian Curtis, Peter Hook and Stephen Morris.

‌



But it was in 1980, following their official split prompted by the death of lead singer Curtis, that their hit Love Will Tear Us Apart really put them on the map. It reached number 13 in the UK singles chart, topping the UK indie chart the following month, and remains an anthem for generations of fans to this day.

‌



In the late 1970s, Joy Division became precursors to a music scene dubbed Madchester, a combination of indie rock, acid house, psychedelia and 1960s pop. Nestling between punk and Britpop, Madchester was driven by bands like The Stone Roses, Happy Mondays and New Order, formed by Joy Division’s remaining members, together with Gillian Gilbert, later in 1980. Inspiral Carpets, another notable act, had a young Noel Gallagher as a roadie, before he formed Oasis.

‌



View 5 Images The Stone Roses posed in a London studio in July 1990. Clockwise from top left: John Squire, Ian Brown, Reni and Mani. (Image: Redferns)

Describing Madchester as “a story that rewired British youth culture”, DJ Steve Lamacq says: “In Manchester, as the 80s turned into the 90s, indie guitars collided with dance music. Rave culture flooded the city, and ecstasy reshaped the dance floor.” On BBC Radio Six Music series, The Rise and Fall of Madchester, which airs ahead of the 6 Music Festival in Greater Manchester (25th – 28th March), he says: “Madchester was an insane time. It was madness. It changed how a generation moved, dressed and sounded. But it was also the sound of a city worn down by years of social and economic decline, rising defiantly to reclaim its voice and leave a legacy impossible to ignore.”

An iconic 1989 broadcast of Top of the Pops on a Thursday night, featuring The Stone Roses and Happy Mondays, with special guest Kirsty MacColl, took Madchester into the mainstream. Lamacq’s co-host Alison Bell says of the TotP broadcast: “Shaun Ryder [Happy Mondays] is twisting and turning like a man who’s forgotten where he’s put his drink down, while singing along to a record on an old stereo. Next to him, Bez is dancing and grinning and shaking his maracas, and to the other side, there’s national treasure, Kirsty MacColl.”

‌



View 5 Images Oasis' Liam Gallagher and brother Noel Gallagher in 1995 (Image: Getty Images)

Joy Division and Factory Records paved the way for the Manchester music explosion. Factory was an independent Manchester record label founded in 1978 by Tony Wilson and Alan Erasmus that featured many of the city’s iconic bands. Journalist and local TV presenter Wilson, who died in 2007, initially turned a club in a rough part of the city into The Factory. Lamacq recalls: “The Russell Club was a West Indian night spot in the shadow of the notorious Hulme Crescent. Outside, burnt-out cars and abandoned mattresses littered the landscape. Inside, it smelled of marijuana and beer.”

After turning it into The Factory, Wilson recalled: “Suddenly we were running a club, sitting on the barrels at the end of the night counting money out.” Devoid of glamour, it was just a room, but it provided a space for trying out new ideas. And in June 1978, it was where Joy Division played their sixth gig.

‌



Lamacq says: “Imagine the scene. On stage, Ian Curtis moves like someone who’s been forced to dance against his own will. His body jerks and stiffens. He’s angular and awkward and utterly compelling. And this is only Joy Division’s sixth gig. The band had formed two years earlier. Curtis, along with Stephen Morris on drums, Bernard Sumner on guitar, and Peter Hook on bass, had been shaken into action by the Sex Pistols’ notorious show at Manchester’s Lesser Free Trade Hall. From this gig, bands soon started to do their own thing. And with a city full of abandoned buildings, there were plenty of places for young people with limited prospects to rehearse and play.”

View 5 Images Ian Curtis of Joy Division (Image: Redferns)

Mike Pickering reviewed the gig for his fanzine Modern Drugs. He says: “I just thought they were amazing, and I think I wrote something that the stage was like a block of ice. And this kind of amazing frontman came out, and yeah, they were just brilliant. It blew me away.”

‌



At the time, Manchester was grey and grim. Lamacq says of Joy Division’s music: “Tense and almost mechanical, it didn’t offer escape or hope. It summoned up the feeling of a city shaking off ghosts of the past.” Realising the band’s brilliance, Wilson and co-conspirator Erasmus turned The Factory from a club night into a record label.

They then began releasing records by acts that had played there including The Durutti Column and Cabaret Voltaire. As the label became established, the pair signed bands without any traditional contract. Wilson said: “We felt music was sacred and wonderful. It shouldn’t be owned by businessmen. Music wasn’t a product, it shouldn’t be promoted or marketed. We should just be a conduit for great art to get to the people.” To convince Joy Division of his dedication, Tony pricked his finger and dripped blood on a piece of paper to seal their deal. Their debut album, Unknown Pleasures, was subsequently released on June 15, 1979 to rave reviews. That year, the band played nearly 100 shows.

View 5 Images The Hacienda club, Manchester, pictured before demolition (Image: Redferns)

‌



On May 18, 1980, two days before Joy Division’s first tour of America, singer Curtis was at home in Macclesfield. Exhausted, the stress of all that intense touring had taken its toll. Estranged from wife Deborah, he’d started drinking heavily before going on stage, and had been diagnosed with epilepsy a year earlier. That night, at home alone, he listened to Iggy Pop’s The Idiot while crafting a heartfelt letter to Deborah and, aged just 23, ended his own life. Lamacq says: “Curtis’ death only seemed to emphasise the bleakness of the time.

Joy Division’s final single, released a month after his passing, went on to become their best-known song. A poignant full stop to their career, Love Will Tear Us Apart went to No13 in the charts and remains one of Manchester’s most defining and moving musical milestones. "That might have been it, except Tony Wilson and the remaining members of the band refused to accept that this story, their story, a story of transition, would end there – resulting in the realisation of a new place where sounds, arts and movement could all exist under the same roof.”

So, in May 1982, Tony and New Order opened The Hacienda club together in a former yacht showroom on Whitworth Street, Manchester. The venue became a cornerstone of the city’s music scene until its closure in 1997. Tony stood on stage next to a white grand piano and welcomed the crowd, saying: “Hit the music, thank you, happy birthday, happy anniversary, thank you for coming.” The night made it possible for Madchester to be born.

Article continues below

*If you’re struggling and need to talk, the Samaritans operate a free helpline open 24/7 on 116 123 or you can email jo@samaritans.org

News.title: Man throws magnet into river and calls police when he reels it back in
News.content:
Man throws magnet into river and calls police when he reels it back in

Nate DeMontigny was magnet fishing from a bridge when he brought up a metal case - but what he found inside meant he had to contact authorities immediately

View 3 Images A man who went magnet fishing made an incredible discovery (Image: YouTube/@‎CapeCodMagnetCrew)

A fisherman was forced to contact police following a shocking discovery whilst reeling in his catch. Nate DeMontigny participates in what's called magnet fishing - where one searches for magnetic items using a Neodymium magnet fastened to rope. He was fishing from a bridge when he pulled up a metal container.

‌



Initially, Nate, who documents his magnet fishing adventures on his YouTube channel Cape Cod Magnet Crew, believed it was a cosmetics case.

‌



Yet, just as he was preparing to dispose of the case in the bin, he chose to open it to check if anything was inside. And what he uncovered prompted him to ring the police.

‌



He found a firearm, specifically a Derringer pistol, a compact weapon famous for its capacity to discharge just one or two rounds.

View 3 Images The gun was found in acase (Image: YouTube/@‎CapeCodMagnetCrew)

The firearm is named after its inventor Henry Deringer. Notably, one Derringer was allegedly used by John Wilkes Booth to murder Abraham Lincoln. Though, because newspapers at the time misspelt the weapon's name, adding an additional r, the name Derringer stuck.

‌



Discussing the discovery with Newsweek, Nate said he had a hypothesis about how it ended up in the river. He explained: "It was just an assumption but based on the fact it still had the tag, I guessed it was stolen and someone got scared about getting caught with it and tossed it."

Nate, upon discovering his find, promptly contacted the local police force who attended to examine the weapon and remove it.

View 3 Images Nate later called the police and handed the gun in (Image: YouTube/@‎CapeCodMagnetCrew)

‌



Nate revealed that Bridgewater's Police Department will retain the gun for around a year to determine if anyone comes forward to claim it before deciding its fate.

Nevertheless, should he be permitted to keep it, Nate confirmed he has intentions to restore and preserve it. He said: "When I do get it back, I hope to clean it up and mount it in a shadow box. I think it is my best find at this point."

Nate stated that it features among some of his finest discoveries during his time as a magnet fisher and mentioned that officers informed him "as long as it comes back clean, I can get it back".

Article continues below

Nate is among numerous magnet fishers across the US. Whilst its main objective is to locate valuable items, it fulfils a secondary function of clearing rivers and lakes of metallic debris that can contaminate or obstruct waterways.

News.title: Call centre worker shares 'best time' to ring for shortest queue
News.content:
Call centre worker shares 'best time' to ring for shortest queue

People could be wasting time and money by phoning at the wrong time of the day

View 2 Images The method could help people speak to an operator faster (stock photo) (Image: Getty)

A call centre worker has shared the best time to phone in to avoid a long queue. While nobody enjoys waiting to speak to an operator, it's not always easy to avoid a long wait time.

‌



However, people could hedge their bets by ringing at certain times of the day that are usually quieter, saving time and money in the process. According to Martin Lewis' MoneySavingExpert (MSE) team, the best time of day to call is when the soaps are on TV.

‌



Keen to help Brits, Martin Lewis' team asked call centre workers to spill their secrets and top tips. The results were shared in a video posted on the consumer advice website and MSE's Instagram. The team explained that 'phoning call centres at the wrong time is costing you.'

‌



In the clip, the team at MSE explained: "Here's why timing your calls right could save you time and money. We asked call centre workers to spill their insider secrets for the best time to phone them up. Here's what they told us.

"A few workers actually recommended ringing up when the soaps were on because that's the lowest call volume time. You also want to avoid calling just before the lines close or even at five minutes to the hour. This is because the worker might be just about to finish their shift and will want to end your call as quickly as possible.

Content cannot be displayed without consent

‌



"So you might not get the best deal. Obviously, lunchtime tends to be really busy, but it's not just the time of day that matters. It also comes down to the week, month or even time of year that you call.

"Mondays and the beginning and the end of the month tend to be busiest too. It's also good to think seasonally. For example, don't call up to sort out your sports channels the day the Premier League starts."

In response to the video, several commenters shared their own tips. For example, a social media user replied: "When I worked in IT for a call centre the call volumes were always lower on a Wednesday. Also a good day to call for a deal." Someone else wrote: "School picking up times if you don't have children."

Article continues below

View 2 Images The advice was shared by call centre workers (stock photo) (Image: Getty)

Another suggestion read: "The early bird catches the worm." Meanwhile, a former call centre worker claimed: "I was told that calls relating to getting money from customers were always prioritised in the queuing system than those that are customer service related.

"So if there's an option to pay for something press that option then if they can't deal with your issue ask them too transfer you to someone who can."

Boxing.title: Jake Paul suffers embarrassing hygiene gaffe during Donald Trump rally appearance as major wardrobe error exposes long-held insecurity
Boxing.content:
YouTuber-turned-boxer Jake Paul was seen profusely sweating in an appearance with US President Donald Trump at a political rally.

Paul joined Trump on stage at Verst Logistics in Kentucky and was given the chance to address the crowd.

The 29-year-old went viral on social media, not for his comments about Trump and American manufacturing, but instead about his appearance.

Many pointed out vast sweat patches that were visible on Paul's beige suit as he spoke to the crowd.

The patches were particularly prominent under his armpits, which were exposed further when he raised his arms to the crowd.

Paul had previously spoken about how sweating had long been a major insecurity of his.

Jake Paul gets on stage at the Trump rally and has the nastiest pit stains I've ever seen



(it also appears the crowd doesn't know who he is) pic.twitter.com/3Yc071tZzf — Aaron Rupar (@atrupar) March 11, 2026

YouTuber-turned-boxer Jake Paul was seen profusely sweating in an appearance with US President Donald Trump

Paul and his brother Logan had promoted his deodorant brand ahead of his fight with Mike Tyson back in 2024

Paul made the revelation back in 2024 when he launched a deodorant brand W, which he and his brother Logan promoted before and after his bout with Mike Tyson.

'Sweating has been a longtime insecurity of mine,' Paul had said when launching the brand.

'If you know me, you know I'm a clean freak. I always want to smell fresh, but training in Puerto Rico's humid climate makes it a real challenge.

'I needed a product that could keep up with my lifestyle — one that smells amazing but isn't full of junk ingredients, and honestly, one that wasn't from 2006.

'That's why I started W… to create products for guys that will boost their confidence and smell amazing, but also don't cost $20 for a stick of deodorant.'

The W deodorants are claimed to offer 24 hours protection, with the brand website stating: 'With doubled scent load, increased hydration and more sweat-blocking power, our new deodorant and body wash are a monumental breakthrough for mankind (or at least for your morning)'.

During the event, Trump signalled that Paul's influence could eventually shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government in the coming years.

'I'm going to make a prediction that you will be, in the not too distant future, running for political office,' Trump told the fighter and the assembled supporters.

Join the debate Did YOU spot Jake Paul's hygiene gaffe?

Trump had brought Paul onstage during a rally on Wednesday in Kentucky

Trump suggested that Paul could shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government

'And you have my complete and total endorsement.'

Paul had expressed his admiration for Trump credited him with teaching him the fundamental values of grit and resilience.

'We need more factories like this thriving all over the country,' Paul added. And I know [Trump's] going to be the one to bring that here to us.

'I know God is with us. I know he wants us on the right side of history, and everyone here has to do their part.

'And God's got us. Trump's got us. God bless.'

Trump returned the praise, telling the crowd: 'He's a great guy. He's a courageous guy and a talented guy. He's a hell of a fighter, too, by the way.'



The endorsement of Paul's fighting spirit comes as the 29-year-old continues to dominate headlines following his endeavours in the boxing ring over recent years.

Boxing.title: Donald Trump gives Jake Paul shock endorsement for political career as boxer joins President at Kentucky rally
Boxing.content:
President Donald Trump brought boxing star Jake Paul onstage during a rally on Wednesday and made a bold prediction about the social media star's future.

The 29-year-old Ohio native, who has transitioned from a YouTube personality to a major force in the combat sports world, stood alongside the president as the crowd in Hebron, Ky roared in support.

During the event, Trump signaled that Paul's influence could eventually shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government in the coming years.

'I'm going to make a prediction that you will be, in the not too distant future, running for political office,' Trump told the fighter and the assembled supporters.

The president didn't stop at a mere prediction, immediately offering his political backing to the 'Problem Child' should he decide to pursue a seat in the future.

'And you have my complete and total endorsement,' the president added, cementing a public alliance with one of the most polarizing and popular figures in modern media.

President Donald Trump brought boxing star Jake Paul onstage during a rally on Wednesday

Trump suggested that Paul could shift from the boxing ring to the halls of government

Paul expressed his admiration for the president and credited him with teaching him the fundamental values of grit and resilience

Addressing the Kentucky crowd, Paul expressed his admiration for the president and credited him with teaching him the fundamental values of grit and resilience.

He told the crowd that the president had taught him the value of 'courage' and to 'never back down from a fight,' a mantra Paul has lived by throughout his boxing career.

'We need more factories like this thriving all over the country,' Paul said, emphasizing his support for the president's economic vision for the American Heartland.

'And I know [Trump's] going to be the one to bring that here to us,' he continued, signaling his belief in a resurgence of American manufacturing.

The fighter also leaned into a spiritual message, telling the audience: 'I know God is with us. I know he wants us on the right side of history, and everyone here has to do their part.'

'And God's got us. Trump's got us. God bless,' Paul concluded, before reflecting on the local spirit of the attendees in the Bluegrass State.

'I feel all the local Kentuckians feel the same way -- you guys have that fight, you guys have that swag. There's a lot of young kids in here. The future of America.'

Trump returned the praise, telling the crowd: 'He's a great guy. He's a courageous guy and a talented guy. He's a hell of a fighter, too, by the way.'

'We need more factories like this thriving all over the country,' Paul said, emphasizing his support for the president's economic vision

Paul's most recent endeavor in the boxing ring saw him take on British star Anthony Joshua

The endorsement of Paul's fighting spirit comes as the 29-year-old continues to dominate headlines following his endeavors in the boxing ring over recent years.

Paul has transformed the boxing landscape by leveraging his massive digital following to create events that have outdrawn traditional championship bouts in viewership and revenue.

His most recent endeavor in the ring saw him take on British heavyweight star Anthony Joshua, which ended in a knockout victory for 'AJ'.



Whether Paul follows Trump's advice and swaps the gloves for a suit remains to be seen.

Boxing.title: Anthony Joshua quits the UK to move to Dubai - even as thousands try to flee amid rising panic in Middle East warzone
Boxing.content:
Anthony Joshua quits the UK to move to Dubai - even as thousands try to flee amid rising panic in Middle East warzone

Boxing superstar Anthony Joshua is quitting the UK to move to Dubai - even though the desert city is now in a warzone with thousands of Britons desperate to get out.

The former heavyweight world champion, 36, has formally confirmed he will now be resident in the United Arab Emirates despite it currently coming under nightly attack from Iranian suicide drones.

Joshua, who was born in Watford of Nigerian heritage, won a gold medal at the 2012 London Olympics and has since been awarded both OBE and MBE honours - but now is quitting his home country.

The surprise move by 'AJ' - as he is known to fans - is revealed in the small print of paperwork he filed on Friday for his £150million business empire in which he is legally required to state the country in which he lives.

And, extraordinarily, the documentation shows that Joshua has pressed ahead with his decision to change his residential status despite the war between the USA and Israel and Iran which has seen neighbouring UAE come under sustained attack.

Anthony Joshua is moving to Dubai - as revealed in the small print of his paperwork

Footage taken from social media shows Saturday's drone strike on Dubai International Airport

As well as the weather and facilities, Dubai is popular with the wealthy because there is no income tax there.

Joshua would have been liable to pay UK tax on his dividend payment of £10.1million in 2024 and £6.3million in 2023.

His company Sparta Promotions also made profits of £20.396million and paid £6.65million in UK tax in 2024.

Joshua confirmed the decision in the same week that recent UK sport emigre Rio Ferdinand and his wife Kate spoke about what it is like to live in Dubai while it is under missile attack.

Those attacks have seen thousands of Britons living in or visiting Dubai when the war broke out left desperate to get out - with some paying as much as £100,000 to secure evacuation on a private jet.

Filings for two of Joshua's companies, Sparta Promotions Limited and 258 Investments Limited, both show the formal change in where he will from now on reside.

Joshua has long been a regular visitor to Dubai where he uses the renowned sporting facilities for holidays and training, as well as promotional work for brands he represents.

The most spectacular stunt in this vein came in 2017 when he undertook a training sparring session on the helipad of the famous sail-shaped Burj Al Arab hotel - a 90-foot wide landing platform suspended 700 feet above the sea.

Passengers in the terminal at Dubai Airport on Saturday after the airport closed again due to drone strikes

He visited the city late last year, dining out with boxing promoter Eddie Hearn, and again last month when he had training sessions with MMA fighter Khabib Nurmagomedov.

The striking decision to shift base to Dubai follows the tragic road accident in Nigeria last year in which claimed the lives of two close friends, personal trainer Kevin 'Latz' Ayodele and long-time therapist Sina Ghami.

Joshua has been in recovery ever since the ordeal and recently posted pictures of rehab work on his ribs via Snapchat.

He captioned the post: 'Rehabilitation protocol. When you go thru certain things, you realise that you are stronger than you think you are.'

The move makes Joshua the latest high-profile wealthy Briton to be based overseas following the likes of Manchester United owner Sir Jim Ratcliffe and EasyJet founder Stelios Haji-Ioannou, who both live in Monaco, and Pimlico Plumbers tycoon Charlie Mullins, who is in Marbella.

The rules on tax for those with ex-pat status are complex.

If individuals spend 183 days or more in the UK they are considered a UK tax resident, but if it is fewer than 16 days they would not be considered resident and eligible for taxes.

For in-between cases, HMRC officials have a complex 'Statutory Residence Test' to determine whether UK tax has to be paid, weighing factors like how much time is spent in each place and how established their ties are in both countries.

Joshua is pictured emerging from the car crash that claimed his friends' lives

Joshua pictured with friends Latif Ayodele (centre) and Sina Ghami, who both died in the crash

So to maximise any tax saving Joshua could be forced to spend barely two weeks a year here.

In December it was revealed that 'AJ' had spent £1.2 million to secure a 15,300 sq ft penthouse, the Sky Palace, in the new Park Lane 101 development in Sultan Haitham City, in Oman, which is 300 miles away.

But it is thought the apartment is an investment property and it is not clear if he has yet settled on a permanent base in Dubai.



There has been speculation that the attacks on Dubai could see property prices there fall by as much as 20%.

Boxing.title: Amir Khan heads to Africa again as boxing promoters battle for control of the sport
Boxing.content:
Amir Khan heads to Africa again as boxing promoters battle for control of the sport

Promoter Amir Khan is staging another boxing card in Africa, positioning himself in a rapidly shifting promotional landscape where global power brokers are increasingly battling for control of the sport.
